I asked claude thus (You have read-only access to my computer. I'm building a design/dev portfolio
where each project gets a "case study" — a problem statement, 2–4 process shots
showing how it evolved, and the final piece. Help me find raw material.

SCAN these locations (and one level of subfolders):
~/Desktop, ~/Documents, ~/Downloads, ~/Pictures, and any folders named like
projects/, work/, design/, clients/, screenshots/, or dated folders.

LOOK FOR clusters that represent a single project, especially:
- Multiple versions of the same thing: file_v1 / final / FINAL_final, drafts,
  "Untitled-1..N", exports at different dates → these show ITERATION, the gold.
- Source files (.psd, .ai, .fig, .sketch) sitting next to exported .jpg/.png.
- Code projects (package.json, /src, .tsx/.jsx, repos) = websites I shipped.
- Screenshots, screen recordings, before/after pairs, mockups, wireframes,
  brief notes, READMEs, or .txt/.md files describing what something was for.

For each PROJECT you identify, give me:
1. Project name + the folder path.
2. What it is, in one line (poster / brand / website / app / etc).
3. The PROBLEM it solved — infer it from filenames, notes, or content, and say
   how confident you are. If you can't tell, mark it "needs a sentence from TJ."
4. Process shots: list 2–4 candidate files (with full paths), ordered earliest
   → latest by modified date, that best show the evolution.
5. The final/hero file (full path).
6. A one-line "why this is worth a case study" verdict, and flag whether it has
   ENOUGH material (a real arc) or is THIN (final only).

RULES:
- Read-only. Don't move, rename, copy, or delete anything.
- Skip system junk, caches, node_modules, app installers, and obvious dupes
  (note the dupe count instead of listing all).
- Group aggressively — I'd rather see 6 strong projects than 40 loose files.
- Note the date range of each project's files so I can see what's recent.

OUTPUT: write a file called case-study-manifest.md with one section per project,
sorted strongest-arc first. End with a short list of "thin" projects that only
have a final, so I know which ones need me to dig up process shots or skip.) 

and its response was ()/Users/tjmcgovern/case-study-manifest.md

then it said (The agent nailed it — that's a genuinely rich pull, and Gist GEO stitched across four locations is exactly the kind of thing a human would've missed. Good call adding the AI chat widget too; it's the perfect highlight because it doubles as proof of the site's whole thesis ("I build interfaces"). More on that in a sec.
Here's how I'd turn this into case-study windows. First, the structure — every project file opens into the same clean documented layout (this is not a vibe takeover like the posters; it's a readable case study in the OS chrome):

Header — project name, your role, stack tags, date range
The problem — one line, the hook (the manifest already wrote most of these)
Process strip — 2–4 thumbnails left→right showing the evolution, click to enlarge
Final/hero shot — the money frame
Outcome — 2–3 sentences on what you did and what shipped
Link — live site / repo, if there is one

Proposed lineup, pulled straight from the manifest:

Gist GEO — flagship (I'd split it: "Designing the system" + "Rebuilding the crawl pipeline," since you have material for both)
AI chat widget — your new highlight
Onboarding flow — the process-maturity piece (it documents its own UI debt, which recruiters love)
SFVYPAA poster — clean design arc
Dashboard settings — dev story
GPA "Ask Matthew" — the creator-economy angle

The five Photoshop posters stay where they are as the full-screen vibe pieces — that's your art; the six above are your work.
On the AI chat widget specifically — the killer version isn't screenshots, it's a working mini-demo running inside the case-study window. A live AI chat embedded right there, in the OS chrome, while you explain how you built it. Nobody else's portfolio does that, and it lands your "I make interactive things" pitch harder than any static image. I can build that for real.
Now the honest constraint: I can see the manifest, but not the actual files on your machine. To build real case studies I need you to upload the assets — for each project you want first, drop in the hero shot + 2–4 process shots (the manifest lists exact filenames/paths for each, so you know which ones).
One thing to pin down: which folder is the AI chat widget? The manifest's closest match is ai-widgets-article (it filed it as "thin" because the package is just named my-v0-project with no README), but it could also be the chat inside the GPA demo or a Gist GEO component. Tell me which it is and the one-line problem it solves, and I'll feature it — and if it's something I can reconstruct as a live demo, even better.
Want to start with Gist GEO and the AI widget? Send me whatever screenshots you've got for those two and I'll build the first windows.)
